reforms.description: Austria aims to support venture capital investment and reduce tax disincentives for equity investments to enhance the external financing of start-ups and small firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to venture capital, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to increased economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to support venture capital investment was announced as part of the broader economic strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the financing landscape for start-ups, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Continue to support venture capital investment and reduce tax and other disincentives for equity investments.

reforms.description: Austria is increasing the research premium from 12% to 14% of all R&D-related expenditures starting in 2018, aimed at incentivizing private sector investment in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to enhance private sector R&D investment, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in the research premium was announced as part of the 2017 policy package.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing R&D incentives, likely improving the overall R&D landscape in Austria.
reforms.source_quote: Increasing the research premium from 12% to 14% of all R&D-related expenditures from 2018 onwards.

reforms.description: Austria is expanding public support to business angels and allocating further resources to seed financing and university spin-offs to enhance the startup ecosystem.
reforms.component_name: Support for Business Angels and Seed Financing
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to strengthen the startup ecosystem by facilitating access to early-stage funding, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The expansion of public support to business angels was included in the 2017 policy package.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms for startups, which can lead to increased innovation and job creation.
reforms.source_quote: Expanding public support to business angels, allocating further resources to seed financing and university spin-offs.

reforms.description: Austria is encouraged to create a more supportive regulatory environment for FinTech innovation by introducing proportionality in regulatory obligations, which could facilitate the entry of smaller service firms and boost competition and innovation in the banking sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing regulatory barriers, the reform is expected to enhance competition and innovation, leading to improved services and potentially higher productivity in the banking sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Austria could follow suit in this area.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a significant area of the economy, aiming to enhance the competitive landscape for FinTech, which is crucial for modern banking.
reforms.source_quote: Austria could follow suit in this area.

reforms.description: Austria's Digital Roadmap aims to make the country a leading digital business location by establishing a cutting-edge broadband and mobile digital infrastructure, supporting R&D in digital technologies, and improving conditions for start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in digital infrastructure and supporting start-ups is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy aims at making Austria a leading digital business location.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Roadmap represents a comprehensive strategy to enhance Austria's digital capabilities, which is critical for future economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The strategy aims at making Austria a leading digital business location.

reforms.description: The Austrian authorities have confirmed their commitment to fostering a startup ecosystem, aiming to free up a new wave of startups to reactivate the modernization of the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging startups can lead to increased innovation, job creation, and economic dynamism, which supports overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have confirmed their commitment to this goal.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the startup landscape, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Freeing-up a new wave of startups in Austria is crucial for reactivating the modernization of the business sector, and the authorities have confirmed their commitment to this goal.

reforms.description: The Austrian government announced an innovation package aimed at connecting all schools with high-speed broadband internet by 2020 and creating an education foundation to support promising R&D and innovative projects in schools.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure in schools enhances access to educational resources and supports the development of digital skills, which are crucial for future labor market participation.
reforms.status_evidence: The innovation package aims to connect all schools with high-speed broadband internet by 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in educational infrastructure that is expected to have long-lasting effects on the quality of education and the development of digital skills in Austria.
reforms.source_quote: The innovation package aims at connecting all schools with high-speed broadband internet by 2020 and foresees the creation of an education foundation that supports promising R&D and innovative projects in schools.

reforms.description: Austria has established a National Cluster Platform to coordinate cluster development and enhance collaboration between national, regional, and local stakeholders, facilitating the creation of educational institutions that align with local skills demand.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between education and industry, the initiative enhances the skill alignment with labor market needs, thereby supporting productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Cluster Platform was established to coordinate cluster development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the connection between education and industry, which is crucial for addressing skill shortages and improving labor market outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: In 2008, the Federal Ministry of Economy, Family and Youth established a National Cluster Platform to co-ordinate cluster development and bring together national, regional and local stakeholders.

reforms.description: The government should engage in a dialogue with social partners to raise awareness and define a regulatory framework for a modern work environment that may also include a right to disconnect as recently introduced in France.
reforms.package_name: Inclusive Labour Markets in the Digital Era
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Creating a modern work environment can enhance productivity and innovation by allowing for flexible work arrangements.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should engage in a dialogue with social partners.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to modernize work practices, which is significant for adapting to digital changes in the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: The government should engage in a dialogue with social partners to raise awareness and define a regulatory framework for a modern work environment.

reforms.description: The government should continue to modernize ICT-related curricula and teaching methods in schools to better prepare students for the digital economy.
reforms.package_name: Inclusive Labour Markets in the Digital Era
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing ICT education will build a skilled workforce capable of driving innovation in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should continue to modernize ICT-related curricula and teaching methods in schools.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant for aligning education with the needs of the digital economy, impacting long-term human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: The government should continue to modernize ICT-related curricula and teaching methods in schools.

reforms.description: Austria announced a comprehensive Digital Roadmap in January 2017 aimed at integrating different sectoral policies and coordinating across stakeholders to enhance digitalisation in the economy and society.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The roadmap aims to foster innovation and productivity through digitalisation, which can enhance economic growth and social cohesion.
reforms.status_evidence: The Austrian government presented a Digital Roadmap in January 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Roadmap represents a structural change in Austria's approach to digitalisation, aiming to position the country among innovation leaders and address significant gaps in digital skills and infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: The Austrian government presented a Digital Roadmap in January 2017, 'to shape the path towards a digital future and position Austria amongst the innovation leaders in digitalisation.'

reforms.description: Austria introduced a 'Digital Roadmap' in 2017 at the federal government level to accelerate digital transitions and anticipate their disruptive impacts.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the digital capabilities of firms, which can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The Digital Roadmap was introduced at the federal government level.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Roadmap represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing Austria's digital infrastructure and capabilities, which is crucial for maintaining competitiveness in a rapidly evolving global economy.
reforms.source_quote: A 'Digital Roadmap' introduced in 2017 at the federal government level reflects this awareness and the shared policy objective of accelerating the digital transitions.

reforms.description: Austria introduced a 'Start-Up Package' to enhance financing options for technology start-ups by doubling the investments of eligible business angels, thereby addressing the financing constraints faced by new and innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to finance, the reform encourages the establishment and growth of innovative firms, which can enhance productivity and economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The Start-Up Package was introduced to support technology start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances the financial landscape for start-ups, which is crucial for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in Austria.
reforms.source_quote: Austrian policymakers try to diversify the sources of finance for small young firms beyond traditional bank loans with a 'Start-Up Package' topping up (doubling) the investments of eligible business angels into technology start-ups.

reforms.description: The 'Open Innovation Strategy for Austria' was launched to expand the innovation system and improve digital literacy among innovation actors through new forms of cooperation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration and enhancing digital skills, the strategy aims to strengthen the overall innovation ecosystem, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Open Innovation Strategy was launched to improve the innovation system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift towards a more collaborative and inclusive approach to innovation, which is essential for adapting to rapid technological changes.
reforms.source_quote: An 'Open Innovation Strategy for Austria' was launched in 2016 by the Ministry of Transport, Innovation and Technology and the Ministry of Science, Research and Economy 'to expand and develop the innovation system and to improve the digital literacy of innovation actors through new forms of cooperation.'

reforms.description: Austria has introduced a new programme called sme.digital to foster ICT adoption and digital change among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). This initiative aims to enhance the digital capabilities of firms, particularly in light of the significant differences in ICT adoption rates across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital capabilities in SMEs, the programme is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was recently introduced to foster ICT adoption.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative addresses a critical gap in digital adoption among SMEs, which is essential for enhancing overall economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: A new programme sme.digital was recently introduced to foster ICT adoption and digital change.

reforms.description: Austria's 'Austria as a Number One Start-Up Country' initiative aims to stimulate the renewal of business demographics by addressing the specific policy needs of start-ups, which often lack resources to engage in policy processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on the unique needs of start-ups, the initiative is expected to enhance innovation and entrepreneurship, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Policymakers aim at stimulating the renewal of business demographics through this initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it targets the start-up ecosystem, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Policymakers aim at stimulating the renewal of business demographics through a 'Austria as a Number One Start-Up Country' initiative.

reforms.description: Austria's Digital Roadmap includes recommendations to integrate a Digital Skills Plan, which aims to enhance ICT skills among small firm owners and managers, thereby fostering innovation and competitiveness in the digital economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital skills, the reform enhances the capacity of small firms to innovate and compete in the digital economy, which can lead to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Digital Roadmap has been put into action with specific recommendations.
reforms.importance_rationale: The integration of a Digital Skills Plan is a significant step towards enhancing the capabilities of small firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the digital landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Integrate a Digital Skills Plan in the Roadmap, including for small firm owners and managers.

reforms.description: The Austrian government is encouraged to develop technology transfer programmes for businesses, particularly for SMEs, to enhance their innovation capabilities and competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating technology transfer, the reform aims to enhance the innovation capacity of SMEs, which can lead to increased economic growth and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to develop technology transfer programmes has been included in the Digital Roadmap.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for enhancing the innovation capabilities of SMEs, which are vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Develop further the technology transfer programmes for businesses, in particular for SMEs.
